verdicts.note: Evidence confirms the product is named 'Firecrawl'.
Evidence consistently references firecrawl.dev as the main website.
Evidence shows Firecrawl is a web scraping/crawling API service, fitting 'Data, SEO and Scraping'.
Evidence confirms web scraping/crawling to LLM-ready markdown/JSON, self-serve API key signup, free tier, keyless mode, and references to MCP server documentation.
Evidence confirms Bearer token format 'Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR-API-KEY' obtained from Firecrawl dashboard.

Evidence mentions WorkOS ID-JAG for agent credential registration but does not describe OAuth2 as a standard auth method for developers. The MCP server OAuth mention is unclear. This appears to be a specialized agent integration, not standard OAuth2.
Evidence confirms self-serve-free tier with 1,000 monthly credits via signup or keyless mode.
Evidence confirms 1,000 monthly credits free tier, keyless mode (no account, IP-capped, basic scrape/search only), and paid plans starting at $16/month.
Evidence shows endpoints for scrape, parse, batch/scrape, crawl, map, extract, search, deep-research/agent, browser sessions, and billing/usage, justifying 'medium' breadth.
Evidence confirms listed endpoints and mentions Python and Node SDKs.

The URL provided (https://docs.firecrawl.dev/mcp-server) points to Firecrawl's own documentation about MCP server usage, not to a dedicated third-party MCP server repository. The evidence shows Firecrawl exposes an MCP endpoint at https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/v2/mcp but this is Firecrawl's own service, not an independent MCP server project.
The URL points to Firecrawl's documentation about using their service via MCP protocol, not to an independent MCP server implementation. This should not be marked as exists='yes'.
Evidence confirms REST API available with self-serve signup (free tier or paid), making it buildable-now.
No blockers evident; API is accessible with free tier or keyless mode.
All evidence URLs are valid and support the claims made.
0.9 confidence is appropriate given the comprehensive evidence.

revised.name: Firecrawl
revised.one_liner: Firecrawl is a web scraping/crawling API that turns websites into LLM-ready markdown/JSON, with self-serve API key signup, a free tier, and keyless rate-limited access.

auth_methods.notes: Bearer token format: Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR-API-KEY, obtained from Firecrawl dashboard

revised.self_serve_notes: Free tier with 1,000 monthly credits available via signup (API key) or fully keyless mode (no account needed, IP-capped, basic scrape/search only). Paid plans start at $16/month for higher limits.

api_surface.notes: Endpoints for scrape, batch/scrape, crawl, map, extract, search, deep-research/agent, browser sessions, and billing/usage. SDKs available for Python and Node. MCP protocol endpoint available at https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/v2/mcp

evidence.supports: Bearer token auth via Authorization header, API key from dashboard, REST endpoints list (scrape, parse, crawl, map, search, agent, browser)
Full REST API endpoint list including scrape, batch/scrape, crawl, map, extract, search, deep-research, billing; API key signup process
Free tier with 1,000 monthly credits, keyless mode, paid plans starting $16/month
Keyless mode allows CLI/MCP/SDK usage without API key or account, capped per IP
Python SDK usage with API key or FIRECRAWL_API_KEY env var
